premises: There are many animals in the forest. Some squirrels are brown. Some brown animals are fast climbers. The squirrels like to hide nuts. The forest has tall trees.

premises: In a library, there are many books. Some books have blue covers. Some books with blue covers are mysteries. The library has a reading room. The librarian is very helpful.
label: True

premises: If it is raining heavily outside, then the ground will be wet. Today, it is not raining heavily outside. The sun is shining brightly. Children are playing in the park. The birds are singing.

premises: If you study very hard for the test, you will get a good grade. Maria did not study very hard for the test. She watched TV all evening. She went to bed early. She ate a big breakfast.
conclusion: Maria will not get a good grade.
label: Uncertain

premises: If a plant receives enough sunlight, it will grow green leaves. The plant on the windowsill does not receive enough sunlight. The window faces north. There is a tall building blocking the sun. The plant looks a bit yellow.
conclusion: The plant will not grow green leaves.
label: Uncertain

premises: If it is snowing outside, then the temperature is below freezing. The temperature is below freezing. The ground is white. People are wearing heavy coats. The heating is on.

conclusion: There exists at least one unicorn that has a magical horn.
label: Uncertain

premises: Every student in the school has a locker. Each locker is assigned to exactly one student. The lockers are in the hallway. Students use their lockers every day. The school has many lockers.
conclusion: There is a locker that belongs to every student.

conclusion: There is a jersey number that is worn by every player.
